author: Mohamed Diomande
name: Anticipatory Transformer V2: MoE Trajectory Routing + Entropy-Weighted Loss
blurb: Builds on SOTA stack (10L, Int5-MLP/Int6-Attn, BigramHash 10240, SmearGate, OrthoInit, MuonWD, SWA, sliding window eval, zstd-22) with three innovations from anticipation geometry: (1) MoE trajectory routing with 10 specialist experts mapping 7 hidden-state-derived scalars to per-head attention modulation via learned centroids. (2) Entropy-weighted training loss (alpha=0.15). (3) 7-scalar trajectory extraction from hidden state dynamics. Total MoE overhead: ~6.9K params. torch.compile(fullgraph=True) verified stable across all 3 seeds.
notes: 3-seed validated on 8x H100 80GB HBM3 SXM. Mean val_bpb=1.14733 (std=0.00088). MoE trajectory routing adds ~14% per-step overhead (94.67ms vs ~81ms baseline), reducing total steps from ~7379 to ~6338 in 600s. The trajectory innovations do not overcome the step-count deficit in this budget. All innovations independently toggleable via env vars. Code LZMA-9+base64 compressed: 63.7KB -> 20.5KB.
